USB input routing test

Only the explicitly selected USB keyboard and mouse should change this page.

Emergency stop / disable routing

Press every key on the same routed USB keyboard. The routing agent stops and viewer input returns.

Ctrl + Alt + Shift + F12
Waiting for routed input
Move, click, scroll, or type
with the routed USB devices
last=waiting held=none clicks=0 moves=0 wheel=0 pointer=-,-

Isolation checks

  1. The built-in keyboard must still control Windows, but must not change this page after the USB route is active.
  2. The Bluetooth mouse must still move the Windows cursor, but must not increment this page's counters.
  3. The routed USB keyboard and mouse must update the live panel.
  4. The emergency chord on the built-in keyboard must do nothing.
  5. The same chord on the routed USB keyboard must stop routing.

Prototype limitation: routed USB devices are observed, not suppressed, so they still produce ordinary Windows input too.
